name: =?utf-8?Q?Michael=20Guest?=
subject: 1 WEEK UNTIL ELECTION DAY!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/a2b7ecae8712/1-week-until-election-day?e=6645c92bcf)
---
| |
---
| | Dear Friend,

The Republican primary election is only ONE week away!

While the national Democrat candidates for President are in chaos deciding which one is the biggest socialist, on Tuesday, March 10, I’m asking for your help so we can have a big Republican turnout—and send a message that Mississippi still believes in conservative values.

As a reminder, March 10 is during Spring Break week for many people so if you’re going to be out of town that day, please make sure to vote by absentee ballot between now and March 7 at 12:00 noon (the deadline for absentee voting). To find out more details about absentee voting, please contact [your local circuit clerk.](https://michaelguest.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ae3ece7e42a6ae24d22e0094b&id=ba5e403e9d&e=6645c92bcf)

I appreciate your giving me the opportunity to serve you in Congress, and I would be honored to have your continued support in this campaign. I’ve always strived to make you proud and to represent our shared values of faith, family and freedom.

In Congress, I’ve voted with President Trump’s positions[ 97% of the time](https://michaelguest.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ae3ece7e42a6ae24d22e0094b&id=540839e055&e=6645c92bcf) to support free market economic principles, reduce regulations, rebuild our military and secure the border.

I hope you will [join our campaign](https://michaelguest.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ae3ece7e42a6ae24d22e0094b&id=f438ca37a3&e=6645c92bcf) today so I can continue working on your behalf in Congress.

Election Day is just one week away on March 10th, and I hope you will ask your family and friends to vote in this important election. Thank you!

---
| Sincerely,
---
|
---
| Michael Guest
United States Congressman

| In the meantime, we want to make sure you hear [Heidi’s interview with Nathan Robinson of Current Affairs](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=11eb676804bfa0f7eac2ca97a&id=d0d7052c8d&e=c169cb0a8e). Heidi speaks about her background, farming, the Paid Sick Days campaign in Austin, and what it feels like to organize in a red state. In light of [billionaire Bloomberg’s ignorant comments about farmers](https://gmail.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=11eb676804bfa0f7eac2ca97a&id=b615dcc7ff&e=c169cb0a8e), we thought we’d share how **Heidi answered a question about what people don’t know about folks in her profession:**

_“I think maybe the first thing, and this is true for so many people, more than it is even for me, that farmers do a lot more than grow food. That most farmers can rebuild their tractor, that most farmers can write a spreadsheet for crop rotation that would blow your mind, that most farmers are in charge of the sales of their farm, that most farmers are also raising their families onsite, and keeping ties with the community.**That farmers are an exceptionally gifted and hardworking group of people,** and I think that we sometimes just focus on one aspect of that work. Whereas, they, everyday, are running a whole ecosystem to itself.”_
---
| **We have the power to get this farmer who respects all workers into Congress.** We’re so grateful for your support in these last few days before the Primary.
We deeply appreciate everything you bring to this movement.
In solidarity,

Team Heidi

| | Peter,

We emailed you yesterday asking for your help the day before Election Day.

With less than 24 hours until the final day of voting starts, we need your help NOW to make sure we get our final digital ads online. We HAVE to turn out as many people as possible to vote for Christy tomorrow and avoid a run-off.

**Rather than spending the next 10 weeks stuck in a run-off, if we can hit 50% on Tuesday Christy can immediately get to work for the people of this district.** And these digital GOTV ads are our last chance to turn out as many voters as possible.

There's too much on the line to not act now, Peter.

subject: Defense Secretary: ‘This is Our Chance to Bring Troops Home from Afghanistan for Good’
body:
After the 9/11 attacks, the U.S. military deployed to Afghanistan to fight terrorists and “remove the safe haven that the Taliban offered them. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=903c271be4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Defense Secretary: ‘This is Our Chance to Bring Troops Home from Afghanistan for Good’**

After the 9/11 attacks, the U.S. military deployed to Afghanistan to fight terrorists and “remove the safe haven that the Taliban offered them. For more than 18 years, America has been protected,” Defense Secretary Mark Esper writes in _The Washington Post_.

“But this success has come at a high price. Nearly 3,000 U.S. and allied troops have been killed in action, thousands more have been wounded, and we have spent more than $1 trillion to keep us safe and help the people of Afghanistan.”

Over the weekend, “we achieved a promising milestone to bring the war in Afghanistan to a responsible end with the signing of an agreement between the United States and the Taliban. It is a road map to peace, security and stability that, while fraught with many imperfections and uncertainties, is supported by the Afghan government and our NATO allies. Thanks to President Trump’s leadership, it is the best chance we have ever had to end this conflict.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c5d81585bb&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The U.S. has made a decisive move towards a negotiated peace plan in Afghanistan.” Now, all parties must live up to the terms. “We have seen the president walk away from deals where the other party changes the terms at the last minute or does not comply with the agreement,” Army veteran and defense expert Cory Mills writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9372807c5e&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| Last year, President Trump called Chicago the “worst sanctuary city in America.” Here’s another example of why: Recently, an illegal alien who was “arrested for allegedly sexually abusing a toddler would have been in federal custody had the Chicago Police Department in 2019 honored an immigration detainer on him and not [released] him back into the community,” Jason Hopkins reports for [_**The Daily Caller**_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d0a3d45c54&e=3ee4985eea "https://dailycaller.com/2020/02/29/chicago-detainer-illegal-immigrant-child-sexual-assault/").
---
|
---
| While activists march, the Trump Administration gets results. Take January’s Women’s March: The protesters can celebrate that during President Trump’s first two years in office, the number of women in poverty declined by 1.5 million—even as the U.S. population continued to grow, Mary Vought writes in [_**RealClearPolitics**_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fd54e93831&e=3ee4985eea "https://www.realclearpolitics.com/articles/2020/02/29/has_trump_helped_women_let_me_count_the_ways.html").
---
|

clean_body: The paths to beating Donald Trump and unseating Mitch McConnell are the same. I am proud to announce my endorsement of Bernie Sanders for President of the United States. The paths to beating Donald Trump and unseating Mitch McConnell are the same. We need a strong progressive agenda that will inspire and excite millions of new voters to turn out for the first time. And we need a grassroots movement that will channel the energy of working people desperate for change. Bernie Sanders understands that, and that’s what makes him the best candidate to ensure Donald Trump is a one-term president. = = Bernie’s success in this race is a reminder that inspiring voters and giving them something to vote for is what wins. Bernie knows we can’t play politics anymore. He knows that we can’t accept leadership that ignores structural racism and inequity. He knows that healthcare is a right, college should be free, and that we won’t defeat corporate greed by running as a bland moderate. Tomorrow is a big moment for our movement, and I hope you’ll chip in: = This is our chance to take our power back, and we are not going to wait. Last night it was confirmed that Florida had its first case, and the individual in question lives in Manatee and is receiving care at a Sarasota hospital. The state and our local governments are working together with the Department of Health, the CDC, and our hospitals to contain the virus and ensure every person has access to diagnostic tests and treatment. With that said, we all have a responsibility to help contain the virus. Wash your hands regularly (especially before eating, or after coughing or sneezing), stay home if you are sick, and disinfect frequently touched objects with a regular household cleaning spray. If you experience symptoms of the Coronavirus, **contact your physician or the Florida Department of Health at 1-(866) 779-6121**. The Department of Health recommends that you call your healthcare provider before you go in if you are experiencing symptoms, so they can prepare for your visit. If you have questions about symptoms or about known cases, please visit the dedicated Coronavirus website at [www.FloridaHealth.gov/COVID-19](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/15646492/165307410/-1792764972?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83ODIwMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2NTllYTViOC1kYjVjLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwMzllNzQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=nuAYFQh6i29OeZIMn-7jbhBEU90zCBUybWiQXOq5kpI=&emci=de10dd66-d05c-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&emdi=659ea5b8-db5c-ea11-a94c-00155d039e74&ceid=9814644). I know that this is a scary and uncertain time, but I want to reiterate that while cases have been found in Florida, the threat to the public remains low — so long as we all do our part to practice safe virus prevention methods — wash your hands; stay home if you are sick; disinfect frequently touched surfaces. I’ll do everything I can to keep you up to date on the latest developments. Wishing you all good health,
Margaret

name: Rita Hart
subject: SURVEY: Education
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/ce9JzmblnG22soaKkBnAcLQbvMt4aS2puZiL70Lr-64=/q7OaBhuRnkQ0HqwZJZCNw6u9hMK9BwCFQQPCk43W4AM=) I taught English in small rural schools for 20 years. I am passionate about ensuring that every student has the best opportunity to succeed and that we recognize the gifts and abilities of every child. For a long time here in Iowa, we could take pride that our schools were consistently among the very best in the nation. Unfortunately, in the last several years, thanks to poor choices and out-of-touch priorities, our schools have consistently been underfunded. We have seen the effects: larger classes, fewer class offerings and teachers having to reach into their own pockets to pay for supplies. How we think about education is a clear difference in this race. Recently, Mariannette Miller-Meeks, one of my Republican opponents, said that she "doesn't know what fully funding schools means." That's because she's not listening. She is out of step with what I'm hearing from parents and teachers across Iowa -- that our schools are facing serious challenges because of underfunding and misplaced priorities from politicians. **[I'd like to hear from you -- what does a fully-funded school look like to you? What resources do you think our students, parents and teachers need?](https://act.myngp.com/el/ce9JzmblnG22soaKkBnAcLQbvMt4aS2puZiL70Lr-64=/NylwUfriWAY8CpkJ5k4OThQBqaWpcj0IpSp6VUjlTlA=)** **[What does a"well-funded" school look like to you?](https://act.myngp.com/el/ce9JzmblnG22soaKkBnAcLQbvMt4aS2puZiL70Lr-64=/qO97-WXUzmFu9dqx7NYL_4f2-9YYr1WPHoSEVkVggrs=)** We need leaders who prioritize our schools and listen to our teachers, parents and students. We should be putting money, at both the federal and state level, into education, not tax breaks for corporations, millionaires, and billionaires. Everywhere I go in the Second District, teachers, parents, and students are raising the alarm about the consequences of the wrong priorities and the effects of leadership from people like Betsy DeVos instead of advocates for public education. We can do better. It starts with listening to the right voices on education policy: parents, students and educators. **[I sincerely want to know what you think our schools need. Let me know what"fully-funded schools" means to you.](https://act.myngp.com/el/ce9JzmblnG22soaKkBnAcLQbvMt4aS2puZiL70Lr-64=/NylwUfriWAY8CpkJ5k4OThQBqaWpcj0IpSp6VUjlTlA=)** Thank you,

name: Jason Yates
subject: Your Guide to Vote Tomorrow
body:
| | Peter, Tomorrow you can make an exponential impact with your vote. There are important primaries taking place at the federal, state, and local levels. Virginia is one of fourteen states holding elections tomorrow, Tuesday, March 3. Primary elections can feel overwhelming because of how many choices are on the ballot, but your vote is the best opportunity to select the candidates who align with our values. **Here is your simple guide for the Virginia primaries tomorrow:**
1. **Pray and ask the Lord to give you wisdom** as you consider the decisions on your ballot and hold to God’s promise to give wisdom to all who ask of Him (James 1:5).
2. **Use our**[**Voting Assistance Center**](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iUNGKee3_hgG2DzKC9zCC91AcWR37Aq_zM-Pb8UXzJ18RDXQnLcn6XY3v7CgASEi0A&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200302super_tuesday_01&n=1) to find all the information you need to vote, specific to Virginia. We’ve got your polling location info, state resources and more!
3. **Help one other Christian vote in the primary.** Take just a few minutes to email, call, or text another believer and encourage them to vote. Share with them the Voting Assistance Center and remind them why voting is critical in the primaries.

Your vote is not only for yourself; it’s for your family and your community. Galatians 6:10 says, “As you have opportunity, let us do good to all people...” [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66iUNGKee3_hgG2DzKC9zCC91AcWR37Aq_zM-Pb8UXzJ18RDXQnLcn6XY3v7CgASEi0A&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200302super_tuesday_01&n=2) **Do Good. Go Vote.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200302super_tuesday_01&n=3)

clean_body: Peter, In less than 12 hours, voters will start heading to the polls to cast their vote in the Democratic primary. I need your help to get me over the finish line. = = We always say that this is the most important election of our lives — but this year is different. Our country is wealthier than ever, yet inequality is higher than any time since the 1920s. Our climate is in crisis but the President calls climate change a hoax. Our healthcare system is leaving people in crippling debt and is unaffordable for most, but the GOP is trying to kick people off their coverage instead of expanding healthcare to all. We need a bold alternative to their right-wing agenda. I am fighting for Medicare for All, for a Green New Deal, and for an economy that works for everyone, not just the 1%. That is why our primary is critical. We need people in Congress who will fight for equality and justice with everything they have. When we replace Donald Trump in the White House, we need representatives who will fight for progressive values. That is why I need your support — so we can bring real change to Washington. = Fighting for you, Mike Siegel ** = PO Box 2409 Austin, TX 78701

subject: [Polling update] This race is neck and neck
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Markey Committee
body:

# [](https://ma.edmarkey.com/page/m/10c1de34/693b8b0a/430f63ad/72de435e/1025852732/VEsH/?g=-NfBFVNw52hcsCx35ezxeoA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/markey-em-2020?refcode=em_header")

Friends -
Three polls of our race have been released in the last two weeks, and they show the exact same thing: **This race is statistically tied.**
One poll has Ed slightly up, and the others have him slightly down. But all three are within the margin of error.
So here's what matters: This race is neck and neck, and it could go either way.
When Joe Kennedy got in this race, the early polling showed we were down by as much as 14 points. But the gap has closed, and now we must capitalize on this momentum.
That means we've got a lot of work to do on the ground in every corner of Massachusetts if we're going to make sure Ed wins.
[**Make a $3 donation right now to help us capitalize on our momentum and expand our ground game. Ed cannot do this alone. body:
February may be over, but I’m still not over the outpouring of support we saw last month. [ ](http://links.claudiaforcongress.com/u/click?_t=e8d915629893476cb837806f005d02de&_m=0574a8130e804ef69c883f95a68dbc18&_e=754dAaUXXMJdvstyKEsi-8etlQEWEGFoK8aIppNggskrSsq-yWjWHWq4ASFF5AY443FfclDbjO4MfICXu9Tf73E_uJowrdbcmAtHRKM3C6TgDJG0yyQQzRuzqbcijPJCCoHTJC8UT9YTOp5e8-etCnrPSNd31mgCnuW9SMkflKKxlZCMpi8Fk3P3PMNOC88yunHKGJqe_fFfFC9vBcsbQgy0bQUQjsPPoeiXG9BBFRzWpzcjTyUGVGOoV1rsCDmE0canE78k9TJ81SXyQl5z8X5cz8asChUWu5uYi_VvGvMXjzqyWgdIRwtBeGTTckqydsEzk7Q65s8dPaI_jlxxe_FgpmvzhTNG0on6WaBKpCMdPXdtNliJ4w7y5YOzl9UdShhns2hzMdYd5FuNkO6BZA%3D%3D) | |
---
Peter, February may be over, but I’m still not over the outpouring of support we saw last month.

We received major endorsements from President Trump, the New York State Conservative Party, and Elise Stefanik. Then we were able to meet, and beat, our fundraising goals for the month!

None of this would be possible without generous, hard-working conservatives like you.

This election is going to be a tough fight, Peter. Anthony Brindisi has built up a network of ultra-liberal donors from across the country who are pouring millions into this race. Even New York City liberal Michael Bloomberg has helped him out!

_We may not have the support of out-of-state billionaires, but we have the support that matters most: yours._

Anthony Brindisi has failed the hard-working people of Upstate New York by voting for impeachment THREE TIMES, voting with Nancy Pelosi over 90% of the time, and pretending to be bipartisan.

As long as his network of liberal donors is behind him, he’ll be more worried about pleasing them than working for us. That’s why we have to beat him, and why your continued support is so important.

Thank you,
Claudia Tenney
---

# Don't Forget To Vote Tomorrow!
Tomorrow is Super Tuesday, and Minnesota is now part of this extremely consequential day in the presidential primary. If you haven't already taken advantage of early voting, please make sure you have a plan to get to the polls by tomorrow (you can still vote early today if tomorrow won't work for you!). Polling locations will be open until 8pm, Tuesday, and you can find your location using the Secretary of State's Website: [https://pollfinder.sos.state.mn.us/](https://IanToddforcongress.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0824156d3c3076099692cab73&id=87cdc213ff&e=c56804a14d) This primary is incredibly important, so please take part! Regardless of what tomorrow brings, we want all of you to know that our campaign is committed to supporting our Democratic nominee no matter who it may be. We're taking the White House back this year.

body:
President Trump welcomed Colombian President Iván Duque Márquez to the White House earlier today. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9cff6bb5ac&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • March 2, 2020
---
| | |
# **Watch: President Trump gives Coronavirus update**

**President Trump** welcomed Colombian President Iván Duque Márquez to the White House earlier today. Before taking questions in the Oval Office, President Trump gave an update on the federal government’s Coronavirus response.

[President Trump provides update on the Coronavirus response](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a91aa291a4&e=3ee4985eea)

**“Our country** is doing very well. Our professionals are doing, really, an incredible job,” the President said. “We’re also working with other countries to help them because they really have a fear of the unknown.”

**The President** also met this afternoon with pharmaceutical companies that are actively working to develop the COVID-19 vaccine, discussing how the federal government can help accelerate their vaccine development.

**Vice President Mike Pence** , who President Trump tapped to lead the Administration’s Coronavirus response, met with a number of America’s governors today to give an update on the White House Coronavirus Task Force. “We’ll continue to coordinate closely with state & local leaders as we respond,” [he wrote](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4e966ff5ff&e=3ee4985eea) after the meeting.

**A live briefing** from Vice President Pence and Ambassador Dr. Deborah Birx, Coronavirus response coordinator for the White House, will begin at 5 p.m. ET.

[Watch live at 5 p.m. ET: Vice President and Amb. Birx give Coronavirus briefing](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f5b364631d&e=3ee4985eea)

**🎬** [President Trump’s quick, decisive response has saved lives](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=af026f78db&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## 🏈 **Former NFL player Jack Brewer: ‘This President has truly, truly empowered me’**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a66d66f639&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **Last week,** black community leaders from across the country joined President Trump for a White House reception to celebrate African American History Month.

**“We’ve done** Opportunity Zones. We’ve done criminal justice reform. We’ve done a lot of things that people didn’t even think possible,” President Trump said.

**One of those guests** was former NFL player Jack Brewer, who has worked tirelessly his entire life to bring real change to our nation’s inner cities.

**“As a lifelong Democrat—** I ran the NFL Players for Obama for years—and I always wanted policies that helped and served the black community,” Brewer said.

**“This President** has truly, truly empowered me. Seeing him pass the First Step Act, seeing him go into our communities and pass all these policies that are creating jobs for our people, bringing our fathers out of prison . . . I can’t tell you how just motivated I am to continue to work and press forward,” he added.

**“****Thank you,** Mr. President!”

[Watch the President and First Lady celebrate African American History Month](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e02db34747&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|

Wilmot will prioritize fighting climate change, making healthcare a human right, economic opportunity, supporting our veterans, improving our public schools and keeping public lands in public hands. Paid for by A Whole Lot of Folks for Wilmot Collins

name: Mark Warner
subject: 🗳️Make your voice heard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
Tomorrow is the Virginia Democratic Presidential Primary.
|
---
| | Derek,

Tomorrow is the Virginia Democratic Presidential Primary.

No matter who you plan to vote for, it's critical that we all make our voices heard. Participating in the democratic process is one of the best ways to enact change in our communities, the Commonwealth, and across the entire country. If you're frustrated with what's happening in the White House, then join me in casting a vote for change. We can't afford to stay silent -- this is too important.

If you don't already have a plan to vote, **[check out these resources](https://act.markwarnerva.com/page/m/7e85d5d5/53d9897e/5306bc88/1c8986ac/1806639969/VEsH/?g=fQCtPq_HJ6FdDGwS5GeylnA "https://iwillvote.com/?state=va")** to find your polling place and check on your registration status. Remember, as long as you're in line by 7 pm tomorrow night, you'll be allowed to vote.

Tomorrow's election is the first step toward charting our path to a Democrat in the White House and Democratic majorities in the House and Senate come November. I'm taking this responsibility seriously -- and I hope you'll join me in exercising our right to vote tomorrow.

We have the right and the responsibility to vote. It's go time.

-- Mark Warner
